purpose: Closes the discipline gap GPT flagged in BEM-010 item 5: RELEASE_UNKNOWN and RELEASE_LIMITED need hard rules, not just definitions, or they become dumping grounds.

release_unknown_sla.rule: RELEASE_UNKNOWN may not persist for more than 1 audit cycle (one P7 pass) without an explicit blocking_justification entry below. If an area is still RELEASE_UNKNOWN at the next audit cycle without justification, it is automatically force-set to RELEASE_BLOCKING.

current_unknown_areas_and_justification.reason_cannot_resolve_yet: Resolution requires live read of *_stage_runner.py source, not yet performed in any audit session
Individual rule bodies not yet enumerated post-BEM-950

release_unknown_sla.note: Both current UNKNOWN areas already have a justification and an assigned resolution task as of this protocol's creation, so neither is in SLA violation yet. The 1-cycle clock starts at the NEXT P7 audit pass after this protocol exists.

release_limited_registry_rule.rule: RELEASE_LIMITED is only valid if the limitation is listed in accepted_limitation_registry below with operator or external-auditor sign-off. An undocumented RELEASE_LIMITED is treated as a misuse of the status and corrected to RELEASE_UNKNOWN until registered.

accepted_limitation_registry.limitation: No START_CONFIRMED polling — dispatch evidence stops at acknowledgement, not completion
accepted_limitation_registry.accepted_by: NOT YET ACCEPTED — this is the DSM-1 gap, currently tracked as a defect, not an accepted permanent limitation
accepted_limitation_registry.reclassification_note: This row exists to demonstrate the registry mechanism; this particular limitation is NOT eligible for RELEASE_LIMITED sign-off since it is an active blocker, not an accepted scope boundary. It should be reconsidered as RELEASE_BLOCKING once DSM-1 is formally tracked as such everywhere consistently — flagging this as a self-correction for the next SSOT transaction.

accepted_limitation_registry.limitation: provider_router.py TTL-scoped stale-error check and the 3 pre-flight tests from BEM-932 1.B/1.C2 not re-verified after BEM-950 changes
accepted_limitation_registry.reclassification_note: Core cost-policy guard (the part that matters most: paid API block) IS verified and RELEASE_CLEARED-grade; only the older TTL-scoping logic is unverified. Limitation accepted as non-blocking because the higher-priority paid-API block does not depend on it.

self_correction_applied: Per the rule above, 'GD-object governance chain' in architecture_plan_fact_gap.json should NOT remain RELEASE_LIMITED without a valid registry entry. Since its registry entry above is marked NOT YET ACCEPTED, the next SSOT transaction must reclassify this area's release_status to RELEASE_BLOCKING to be consistent with 'Roadmap dispatch' and 'Dispatch lifecycle completeness', which already correctly carry RELEASE_BLOCKING for the same underlying DSM-1 gap.
